intro: Monanthotaxis laurentii is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Central and West Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Monanthotaxis laurentii include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. In traditional medicine, Monanthotaxis laurentii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, with the most common herbal preparations being a tea or a syrup made from the plant's leaves or roots.
